failure_surface: Repeated SyntaxError on line 1 of every execution attempt due to protocol syntax marker appearing as executable Python code
root_cause: The agent incorrectly embedded the function-call protocol marker (<tool_call>function_code_execution) directly within the code block content, causing the Python interpreter to treat non-Python syntax as code.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Code blocks submitted for execution must contain only valid Python syntax; protocol markers and XML-like wrappers must remain outside the executable content boundaries.
evidence: All 4+ execution attempts show identical error: 'Cell In[1], line 1 ... SyntaxError: invalid syntax'
Error arrow points to '<tool_call>function_code_execution' which is not valid Python syntax
The code block structure shows '<tool_call>function_code_execution\n<code>import pandas...' where the marker precedes the <code> tag content
Same logical spreadsheet solution repeated across all attempts, yet execution fails consistently before any Python runs

memory_items.title: Executable Code Purity
memory_items.lesson: Only valid programming language syntax should appear inside executable code blocks; protocol wrappers, comments, or instruction markers must be placed outside the code content.
memory_items.generalization: When constructing code for programmatic execution, verify that no non-executable prefixes or suffixes contaminate the actual code payload.

memory_items.title: Protocol Marker Separation
memory_items.lesson: Function-call syntax markers serve as orchestration commands, not code; they should trigger execution systems rather than be part of the executed script.
memory_items.generalization: Distinguish between execution directives and executable content during response construction to prevent interpreter-level failures.

memory_items.title: Execution Hygiene Validation
memory_items.lesson: Before submitting code, validate that stripping all markup leaves only syntactically correct code for the target interpreter.
memory_items.generalization: Implement a mental or automated check ensuring code blocks are interpreter-ready by removing all wrapper protocols.

edits.content:
### 7. Execution Hygiene
- **Code Block Integrity:** Ensure executable code blocks contain only valid syntax for the target interpreter.
- **Protocol Separation:** Function-call markers, XML wrappers, or instruction prefixes must not appear inside the actual code content.
- **Pre-Validation Check:** Before execution submission, mentally strip all markup to confirm remaining content is pure, runnable code.
- **Syntax Verification:** When receiving repeated SyntaxErrors on line 1, verify that no non-language tokens are contaminating the code block boundary.
